title: Reading and Use of English - Part 6
description: You are going to read an article from which six sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–G the one which fits each gap (37–42). There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.

content.title: The Patient Art: Wildlife Photography
content.text: Before dawn on a Kenyan savanna, a photographer lies flat in the damp grass, camera pointed at a pride of lions three hundred metres away. She has been in position since midnight. She may wait another four hours without taking a single shot. (37) [37] Esta clase de dedicación silenciosa define la fotografía de naturaleza como disciplina. [/37] Wildlife photography is not simply a matter of pointing a lens at an animal; it is a craft that demands biological knowledge, physical endurance and extraordinary patience. || The technical demands are formidable. Wildlife photographers work with long telephoto lenses that magnify the slightest movement, requiring rock-steady technique and expert knowledge of exposure settings in rapidly changing light. (38) [38] Capturar el momento decisivo en condiciones impredecibles exige reflejos rápidos y un profundo conocimiento del equipo. [/38] A lion emerging from shade into sunlight can move from underexposed to overexposed in a fraction of a second, and the photographer must anticipate and respond instantly. || Understanding animal behaviour is equally critical. The best wildlife photographers spend years studying the species they photograph, learning the subtle signals that precede a charge, a hunt or a display. (39) [39] Este conocimiento no solo mejora las fotografías, sino que también protege al fotógrafo de situaciones peligrosas. [/39] An intimate familiarity with a subject's habits allows a photographer to position themselves for an image before the decisive moment arrives, rather than scrambling to react after it has passed. || The ethics of wildlife photography have come under increasing scrutiny. There are well-documented cases of photographers using playback recordings to lure birds, baiting predators with food or using drones that disturb nesting animals. (40) [40] Estas prácticas pueden causar estrés o daño real a los animales que se pretende fotografiar. [/40] Professional bodies such as the Royal Photographic Society have introduced ethics codes that explicitly prohibit such practices and can result in disqualification from competitions. || Conservation photography — a branch of the discipline focused explicitly on documenting threats to wildlife and habitats — has emerged as a powerful advocacy tool. (41) [41] Imágenes de animales en peligro o hábitats destruidos han movilizado opinión pública y financiación para causas conservacionistas. [/41] Photographs of oil-covered seabirds, bleached coral reefs and elephant poaching have driven policy changes and fundraising campaigns in ways that statistics alone rarely achieve. || Technology is reshaping the field in other ways. Camera traps — remotely triggered cameras set up in the wild — allow photographers to capture images of the shyest nocturnal species without any human presence. (42) [42] Estas cámaras automáticas han revelado comportamientos animales que de otro modo nunca habrían sido documentados. [/42] For wildlife photographers, each technological advance opens a new window onto a world that most humans will never directly observe.

paragraphs.A: This kind of silent dedication — hours of stillness for seconds of action — defines wildlife photography as a discipline.
paragraphs.B: Capturing the decisive moment under unpredictable conditions requires fast reflexes and deep technical mastery.
paragraphs.C: This knowledge not only improves the photographs but also keeps the photographer safe in potentially dangerous situations.
paragraphs.D: These practices can cause genuine stress or harm to the very animals the photographer is trying to celebrate.
paragraphs.E: Images of endangered animals and destroyed habitats have mobilised public opinion and funding for conservation causes.
paragraphs.F: Some of the most celebrated wildlife photographs have been taken within metres of a sleeping or feeding subject.
paragraphs.G: These remote cameras have revealed animal behaviours that would never otherwise have been documented.

questions.explanation: A generalises the specific scene of waiting through the night and frames it as a defining characteristic of wildlife photography.
B describes the technical demands of capturing the decisive moment, connecting with the specific example of the lion in changing light.
C adds the double benefit of knowledge of animal behaviour: better photos and greater personal safety.
D points out the real damage that questionable practices can cause to animals, justifying the response of professional bodies.
E specifies the impact of conservation photography in mobilising support, before the examples of birds, reefs and elephants.
G explains the contribution of camera traps in revealing unknown behaviours, closing the article with a future perspective.
